text: In association football, how many players does each team normally start with on the pitch?

What does VAR stand for in football?
Which restart is used when the ball fully crosses the touchline?
What is the maximum number of touches a single player may take during a penalty shootout kick?
Which tournament is commonly known as the Euros?
In football tactics, what does a 'false nine' usually describe?
options: A striker who drops deeper from the forward line
A defender playing offside on purpose
correct_answer: A striker who drops deeper from the forward line

text: Which body writes and maintains the Laws of the Game for football?
What does a red card mean for a player in football?
What is a hat-trick in football?
options: Three goals by one player in a match
Three yellow cards for a team
correct_answer: Three goals by one player in a match

text: Which line marks the halfway point of a football pitch?
How many players from one team are on the court during standard basketball play?
How many points is a successful shot worth from beyond the three-point line?
What is a triple-double in basketball?
options: Double figures in three statistical categories
Three made free throws in a row
correct_answer: Double figures in three statistical categories

text: Which violation is called when a player moves both feet illegally without dribbling?
What is the name of the line where uncontested foul shots are taken?
What does an assist record in basketball?
options: A pass leading directly to a made basket
correct_answer: A pass leading directly to a made basket

text: Which basketball role is usually responsible for organizing the offense and bringing the ball up court?
What is goaltending in basketball?
options: Blocking a shot illegally on its downward path
Standing under the hoop on offense
correct_answer: Blocking a shot illegally on its downward path

text: What is a rebound in basketball?
options: Recovering the ball after a missed shot
Making two baskets in a row
correct_answer: Recovering the ball after a missed shot

text: Which area is commonly called the paint in basketball?
options: The free-throw lane near the basket
correct_answer: The free-throw lane near the basket

text: What is a fast break in basketball?
options: A quick offensive attack before the defense is set
A slow possession to use the clock
correct_answer: A quick offensive attack before the defense is set

text: What does a shot clock limit?
options: How long a team can possess the ball before shooting
How long a player can sit on the bench
How long a referee can review a play
correct_answer: How long a team can possess the ball before shooting

text: What is the first point score called in a standard tennis game?
What is a serve that the receiver cannot touch called?
What score is called when both tennis players have at least three points and the score is tied?
Which Grand Slam is played on grass courts?
Which Grand Slam tournament is famously played on clay courts?
What does 'love' mean in tennis scoring?
What is a break of serve in tennis?
options: The receiver wins a game served by the opponent
correct_answer: The receiver wins a game served by the opponent

text: What is a volley in tennis?
options: A shot hit before the ball bounces
A shot hit after two bounces
A ball hit outside the court
correct_answer: A shot hit before the ball bounces

text: What is a tiebreak used for in tennis?
Which side of the court is the deuce court for a right-handed server facing the net?
What is a double fault in tennis?
options: Losing two points in a row
Hitting two winners in one rally
Serving from the wrong side twice

text: What is the term for a high defensive shot sent over an opponent near the net?
What does a yellow flag usually signal in circuit racing?
What does a checkered flag traditionally indicate in motorsport?
What is a pit stop used for in racing?
options: Servicing the car during a race
correct_answer: Servicing the car during a race

text: In Formula 1, what does pole position mean?
What is understeer in car racing?
options: The car turns less than the driver wants
The rear tires slide outward first
The brakes lock only at the rear
correct_answer: The car turns less than the driver wants

text: What is oversteer in car racing?
options: The rear of the car steps out more than intended
The car gains an extra lap
correct_answer: The rear of the car steps out more than intended

text: What does DNF mean in race results?
What is downforce designed to do on a race car?
options: Push the car toward the track for grip
correct_answer: Push the car toward the track for grip

text: What is a chicane on a racing circuit?
What is slipstreaming in motorsport?
options: Following another vehicle to reduce air resistance
Driving with wet tires on a dry track
Starting from the wrong grid slot
correct_answer: Following another vehicle to reduce air resistance

text: Which flag is commonly used to disqualify or order a driver to stop racing?
What is qualifying used to determine in many racing series?
In boxing, what is a knockout?
options: A fighter is unable to continue after being counted out
A fighter wins by crowd vote
A round ends early for water
correct_answer: A fighter is unable to continue after being counted out

text: Which combat sport uses the terms jab, cross, hook, and uppercut?

What does MMA stand for?
Which combat sport is known as the 'art of eight limbs'?
In wrestling, what is a takedown?
options: Bringing an opponent to the mat from standing control
correct_answer: Bringing an opponent to the mat from standing control

text: What is the main purpose of weight classes in combat sports?
In fencing, which weapon is a thrusting weapon with a valid target limited to the torso?
Which boxing result means the fight ends with no winner due to equal scoring or rules conditions?
What is a southpaw stance in boxing?
options: Right hand and right foot forward
Left hand and left foot forward
Both feet square at all times
A stance used only by referees
correct_answer: Right hand and right foot forward

text: What does FPS commonly stand for as a game genre?

options: A tournament played on a local area network
A tournament using only mobile phones
correct_answer: A tournament played on a local area network

text: What does GG usually mean in competitive gaming?
What is a patch in video games?
options: An update that changes or fixes the game
correct_answer: An update that changes or fixes the game

text: What is a meta in competitive gaming?
options: The commonly strongest or most-used strategies
correct_answer: The commonly strongest or most-used strategies

text: What is an objective in many team-based esports games?
options: A map goal that helps win the match
correct_answer: A map goal that helps win the match

text: What does DPS commonly mean in games?
What is a scrim in esports?
What does ping measure in online gaming?
What is a best-of-three series?
options: The first side to win two games wins the series
A series with exactly three teams
A match that cannot end early
A match played for three hours
correct_answer: The first side to win two games wins the series

text: What is a caster in esports?
options: A commentator who calls the match
A player who only uses magic characters
correct_answer: A commentator who calls the match
